title: Building an HTML-first site doubled our users overnight
author: edent
aiHeadline: Who needs fancy frameworks when a simple HTML page can make your user base explode overnight? Just ask edent, because apparently, going back to basics is the new black in web development.

title: πFS
author: helterskelter
aiHeadline: Mathletes unite! Finally, a filesystem so slow, it'll make you question the meaning of life and your hard drive's purpose. Introducing πFS, because who doesn't want their data stored in an infinite loop of decimals?

title: I'm Eric Ries, author of "The Lean Startup" and new book "Incorruptible" – AMA
author: eries
aiHeadline: The Lean Startup guru is back with another book, and this time it's about being incorruptible—probably because everyone's too busy asking him how to make their startups fail faster. Spoiler: it involves a lot of pivoting and maybe some questionable life choices.

title: Mercedes‑Benz starts large‑scale production of electric axial flux motor
author: raffael_de
aiHeadline: Mercedes-Benz is ditching combustion engines for electric motors, proving that even luxury cars can be environmentally unfriendly in a whole new way. Because what's more sustainable than a $100,000 electric car that still needs a charging station?

title: Farmer donates land for a park, city sells it for $10M as data center land
author: maxloh
aiHeadline: A farmer's generous gift of land for a park turned into a goldmine for the city, which promptly sold it to data centers—because who needs green spaces when you can have greenbacks and servers that need air conditioning? The future is bright, and it's powered by your tax dollars.
